title: Dividend Growth Checkup - 2021
text: Analyzing my real-life portfolio's dividend growth for 2021. Dividend growth rebounded sharply in 2021, which was a more-than-welcomed sight. Weighted dividend growth came in at 8.7%, which is more than adequate.

title: Iron Mountain Closes Acquisition of ITRenew
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Iron Mountain Incorporated (NYSE: IRM), the leading information management company, has completed its acquisition of ITRenew, a global leader in mission critical data center lifecycle management solutions. With the transaction, Iron Mountain acquired 80 percent of the outstanding shares of ITRenew on a cash- and debt-free basis for approximately $725 million in cash, with the remaining 20 percent acquired within three years of close for a minimum enterprise value of $92

title: Iron Mountain Appoints Gillian Tiltman as Senior Vice President and Head of Investor Relations
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Iron Mountain Incorporated (NYSE: IRM), the leading information management company, today announced that it has appointed Gillian Tiltman as Senior Vice President and Head of Investor Relations, effective immediately. In her role, Tiltman will be responsible for devising, executing and leading Iron Mountain's investor relations strategy. title: Why It is Wise to Hold Iron Mountain (IRM) Stock for Now
text: Iron Mountain (IRM) is likely to have benefitted from its focus on data-center portfolio expansion, though a low service activity level is a headwind.

title: B. Riley Financial, Iron Mountain, And Franchise Group - A Forward And Backward Analysis Of Three 5% Yielders
text: RILY, IRM, and FRG all pay a 5% dividend yield as of January 2022, and all have bullish ratings from both Wall Street analysts and SA authors. To help with an investment decision, I compared the historical returns and simulated forward estimates for each of the 3 using Monte Carlo simulation.

title: Iron Mountain: Why This Cash Cow Is Now A Buy
text: Iron Mountain has seen material share price weakness since the start of the year. Its storage business is expanding nicely and management has rolled out innovative service solutions.

title: Iron Mountain: Recent Dip Creates Entry Opportunity For This Perpetual Compounder
text: Iron Mountain suffered a sizable price correction of ~15%, most likely due to concern about its ongoing business transition and debt issue under a rising rate environment. Its traditional paper storage segment is holding up well, though the outlook is pessimistic. However, our needs for paper storage and storage for other physical “stuff” will remain.
